question: Is the wheelchair athlete present and identifiable as the female subject from the reference (short hair, blue shirt)?
Is the wheelchair athlete depicted in a specialized racing wheelchair (angled wheels, low profile) consistent with the reference?
Is the blade runner present and identifiable as the male subject from the reference (red tank top, black shorts)?
Is the prosthetic running blade clearly visible on the blade runner's leg and anatomically integrated?
Is the visually impaired athlete present and identifiable as the female subject from the reference (yellow outfit)?
Is the visually impaired athlete depicted with her guide dog or clearly visible visual impairment aids (sunglasses/tether)?
Is the wheelchair athlete positioned in the nearest lane (foreground) of the track?
Is the blade runner positioned in the middle lane, between the wheelchair athlete and the visually impaired athlete?
Is the visually impaired athlete positioned in the outer lane (background/furthest from camera)?
Are all three athletes facing and moving in the same direction?
Is the perspective scaling accurate, with the wheelchair athlete appearing largest and the visually impaired athlete appearing smallest?
Is the background clearly recognizable as the outdoor red running track from the reference image?
Are all athletes depicted in dynamic racing poses (e.g., leaning forward, sprinting stride) rather than static or casual postures?
Does the lighting on the athletes match the warm, golden-hour direction and tone of the background?
Are appropriate contact shadows visible beneath the athletes/equipment to ground them on the track?
Does the image have an inspiring, high-impact aesthetic typical of a sports promotional poster?
Are the athletes free from severe anatomical distortions (e.g., missing limbs, malformed faces) or structural errors in the wheelchair?
Is the compositing clean, without visible cut-out halos, jagged edges, or mismatched resolution artifacts?
